title: The imminent risk bulletin (BRI): innovation from civil society for climate resilience in Mexico
content: The Imminent Risk Bulletin (BRI) is a civil society-driven impact-based forecasting tool developed in Mexico to bridge the gap between atmospheric science and citizen survival.
snippet: The Imminent Risk Bulletin (BRI) is a civil society-driven impact-based forecasting tool developed in Mexico to bridge the gap between atmospheric science and citizen survival.

title: Uninsurability is a policy choice
content: In California, “uninsurable” means wildfire premiums jumping from $2,500 to $20,000 a year, or insurers refusing to renew policies altogether.  In rural Mozambique, it means families having no financial safety net when a cyclone destroys their home.

title: Unpacking climate change and health nexus in Bangladesh: 10 points on how the country can prepare to implement and support the WHO’s global action plan on climate change and health (2025)
content: This working paper offers a timely and in-depth exploration of the climate-health nexus, specifically focusing on the context of Bangladesh as this country faces some of the world’s highest global climate risks.

title: A giant warm wave is crossing the Pacific, signaling an El Niño that could alter weather worldwide this year
content: Waves of higher, warmer water move eastward across the Pacific Ocean a few months before an El Niño emerges. Several have shown up in 2026 satellite data.
snippet: Waves of higher, warmer water move eastward across the Pacific Ocean a few months before an El Niño emerges. Several have shown up in 2026 satellite data.

title: Fields of thunder Scientists track processes that fuel powerful thunderstorms over U.S. Corn Belt
content: An international team of scientists has demonstrated how powerful thunderstorm complexes over the U.S. Corn Belt are fueled by moisture rising from the region’s fertile fields or just beneath them.
snippet: An international team of scientists has demonstrated how powerful thunderstorm complexes over the U.S. Corn Belt are fueled by moisture rising from the region’s fertile fields or just beneath them.

title: ‘There is no great master plan’: anxiety as UK homes, roads and railways sink into the sea
content: Increasing coastal erosion has hit communities’ livelihoods and put lifestyles under threat.
snippet: Increasing coastal erosion has hit communities’ livelihoods and put lifestyles under threat.

title: Call for input: Hazards media toolkit pilot
content: Help shape a new pilot project by selecting priority hazards and recommending experts for the Technical Working Group. The survey takes 5 to 7 minutes and all responses are confidential.

title: Climate change exposes hundreds of millions to longer and deadlier pre-monsoon heat in South Asia

title: Insights help predict crack formation in clay layers for dykes at an early stage
content: Crack formation in clay layers poses a risk to the safety of dykes, as well as to landfills and other hydraulic infrastructure that rely on clay as a sealing or water-retaining layer. When cracks develop, the risk of failure rises.

title: WMO report suggests more global temperature records ahead
content: Global average temperatures are likely to continue at or near record levels in the next five years, with Arctic temperature anomalies expected to continue to be higher than the global mean.
snippet: Global average temperatures are likely to continue at or near record levels in the next five years, with Arctic temperature anomalies expected to continue to be higher than the global mean.

title: WMO Global annual to decadal climate update 2026-2035
content: The Global Annual-to-Decadal Update takes a look at the observed climate over the past five years and gives regional predictions for temperatures and precipitation over the next five years.
snippet: The Global Annual-to-Decadal Update takes a look at the observed climate over the past five years and gives regional predictions for temperatures and precipitation over the next five years.

title: Heatwaves: older people less likely to follow safety advice
content: Extreme heat is now considered the deadliest weather and climate-related hazard in Europe, causing more deaths than floods or storms.
snippet: Extreme heat is now considered the deadliest weather and climate-related hazard in Europe, causing more deaths than floods or storms.

title: Too hot, too humid: why the sustained heatwave in India and Pakistan is so dangerous
content: India and Pakistan are no strangers to heat. This time of year is the worst, as heat peaks before the monsoon brings cooler conditions from June.
snippet: India and Pakistan are no strangers to heat. This time of year is the worst, as heat peaks before the monsoon brings cooler conditions from June.

title: Anthropogenic climate change accelerates the onset of global flood timing
content: This study provides a global assessment of flood timing changes and associated risks under incremental warming levels.

title: Research shows that prescribed fire, other fuels treatments net major savings
content: It’s well-established that prescribed fire and other fuel-reduction treatments can create significant ecological benefits and reduce the risk of catastrophic wildfire.
snippet: It’s well-established that prescribed fire and other fuel-reduction treatments can create significant ecological benefits and reduce the risk of catastrophic wildfire.

title: A ‘futuristic’ approach to minimizing power outages

title: Spain to launch largest-ever forest fire campaign after record losses

title: Q&A: The Alps are crumbling, and permafrost is not playing the role many assumed

title: The US is seeing stronger storms, so why are droughts getting worse?
content: About two-thirds of the U.S. is in some stage of drought in late spring 2026, yet at the same time the country has been seeing more intense downpours . It might seem contradictory, but both are symptoms of rising global temperatures.

title: Integrating flood risk management is key to building resilient social infrastructure
content: Flood-resilient social infrastructure protects lives, sustains services, and reduces recovery costs.
snippet: Flood-resilient social infrastructure protects lives, sustains services, and reduces recovery costs.

title: Lethal humidity may be one of climate change’s most overlooked threats
content: By late this century, more than 50 percent of humanity could be exposed to life-threatening heat and humidity. But one of climate change’s deadliest emerging risks remains poorly understood: lethal humidity.

title: Fatalities from landslides in earthquakes
content: A study by Sun et al. 2026 shows that in six earthquakes in China between 2010 and 2022, landslides and rockfalls were responsible for at least half of the total fatalities.
snippet: A study by Sun et al. 2026 shows that in six earthquakes in China between 2010 and 2022, landslides and rockfalls were responsible for at least half of the total fatalities.

title: Weak faults play a strong role in the Tibetan plateau’s deformation
content: Ten years’ worth of data reveal that two theories about how the Tibetan Plateau deforms are both probably right.
snippet: Ten years’ worth of data reveal that two theories about how the Tibetan Plateau deforms are both probably right.

title: How London, Paris and New York coped in the heatwaves of the past
content: Paris, London and New York are more often associated with culture, finance and history than with dangerous heat. Yet each summer all three are increasingly exposed to extreme temperatures they were never designed to withstand.

title: Assessing the impact of wildfires on the Swedish housing market: a case study of the 2014 Västmanland wildfire
content: The study investigates how the largest wildfire in Sweden's recent history, the 2014 wildfire in Västmanland County, affected nearby housing prices and time-on-market.
snippet: The study investigates how the largest wildfire in Sweden's recent history, the 2014 wildfire in Västmanland County, affected nearby housing prices and time-on-market.

title: As Ebola resurfaces in DR Congo, so do critical questions about how to respond
